exemplar.title: To what extent has Tesla's sustainability strategy influenced its profitability and strengthened its competitive advantage?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Tesla's sustainability strategy influenced its profitability and strengthened its competitive advantage?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear identification of sustainability as the key concept
Analytical points consistently link Teslaâs practices to sustainability
Contextual examples (battery supply-chain, R&D spend) reinforce application
weaknesses: Some financial commentary drifts into generic profitability without tying back to sustainability
Integration is strong but not fully seamless in every paragraph
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed this at Band 4 for strong understanding and clear analysis, though falling short of fully integrated Band 5. A score of 4/5 reflects solid conceptual integration with minor gaps.

strengths: Four supporting documents meet the 3â5 requirement
Sources are directly relevant to Tesla and sustainability
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on Teslaâs own publications limits critical perspective
Depth is uneven; student extracts limited data from academic/industry sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although four documents are used, depth and independence of evidence are limited. This aligns more closely with Band 2 (âgenerally relevant but lack depthâ) rather than Band 3.

strengths: Adequate selection of tools (financial analysis, SWOT, CSR, stakeholder theory)
Each tool is applied to aspects of profitability or competitive advantage
weaknesses: Application of ratio analysis is minimal, limiting insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments indicate an adequate but uneven application, matching Band 3. The selection is sufficient and relevance clear, meriting 3/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Use of recent multi-year revenue and cost data to support claims
Evaluation of sustainabilityâs impact on margins shows some evaluative thinking
weaknesses: Interpretation often remains at assertion level
Counter-arguments and deeper causal testing are limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection and analysis are adequate but partial, consistent with Band 3. A score of 3/5 reflects solid foundation with room for deeper integration and evaluation.

strengths: Conclusions generally reflect evidence on revenue growth and cost pressures
Explicitly answers the research question about sustainabilityâs extent of influence
weaknesses: Some statements over-generalise or contain numerical inaccuracies
Redundancy in repeated conclusion sections impacts coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions are consistent with evidence overall and directly address the research question, but contain errors and over-generalisation. This aligns with Band 2, scoring 2/3.

strengths: Logical organization with clear headings and TOC
Title page and appendices support structure effectively
weaknesses: Minor transition gaps (e.g. incomplete CSR section transition)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report exhibits appropriate structure throughout, matching Band 2. A full 2/2 is warranted.

strengths: All required elements (title page, word count, TOC, bibliography, appendices) are present
weaknesses: Figure labeling referring to appendices can confuse the reader
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all required elements with minor refinements needed, fitting Band 2. Score: 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows a logical structure with clear headings, TOC and appendices, matching the âappropriate structure throughoutâ descriptor.

**Strengths:** The annual report, impact report, academic article and industry paper are all directly relevant to sustainability and Tesla, providing credible quantitative and qualitative data.  
**Limitations:** Depth is uneven; two sources are Teslaâs own publications, and the student does not extract multiple data points from the academic sources, limiting range and critical depth needed for the highest band.
Title page, word count, table of contents, bibliography and appendices are all present, so all required elements are included.
Four supporting documents are listed, fitting the 3-5 requirement. This meets rubric expectations but does not exceed them (no fifth source to justify the top band).

**Strengths:** Each tool is applied to profitability or competitive advantage, showing clear though sometimes surface-level relevance to the RQ.  
**Limitations:** Application is unevenâSWOT is largely descriptive and ratio analysis is minimalâso effectiveness is adequate rather than consistently clear and insightful.

**Data selection:** Student chooses recent multi-year revenue, cost and margin figures plus market-share chartâan adequate evidence set, though limited in breadth (no profitability ratios, no external benchmarking).  
**Use:** Data are quoted accurately and used to support claims, but interpretation remains basic (e.g. attributing cost increases solely to R&D). This aligns with the âadequateâ level.

**Strengths:** The student analyses how sustainability investment affects margins, discusses stakeholder theory implications and weighs risks, showing some evaluative thinking.  
**Limitations:** Analysis often stops at assertion; causal links are not rigorously tested and counter-arguments are brief, so integration is partial rather than âmostly effectiveâ. Hence a mid-band score is appropriate.
The conclusion explicitly answers âto what extentâ by stating sustainability is a keyâbut not soleâdriver, satisfying the highest descriptor for addressing the research question.

Tools include financial-statement analysis, SWOT, CSR and brief reference to stakeholder theory. This constitutes an adequate but not especially wide or innovative selection, aligning with the âadequate selectionâ descriptor.

Conclusions generally reflect the evidence (e.g. revenue growth balanced by cost pressure), but some statements over-generalise (âexponential increaseâ from 53m to 96m is numerical error) and rely on unsubstantiated predictions. This partial alignment fits band 2.

**Strengths:** Analysis is consistently rooted in Teslaâs specific context, drawing on up-to-date revenue figures, R&D spend, customer-loyalty data and product sales rankings. Insights about battery supply-chain risks and vertical integration further anchor arguments in the organisation.  
**Limitations:** A few evaluative claims (e.g. âthis exhibits Teslaâs commitmentâ¦â) are asserted with limited organisation-specific evidence. The connection is therefore strong but not yet âcomprehensive and fully integratedâ across every section.

**Strengths:** The student clearly identifies sustainability as the key concept and weaves it through all major sections (financial analysis, CSR, SWOT and conclusion). Each analytical point explicitly links back to how Teslaâs sustainable practices influence profitability and competitive advantage, showing solid conceptual understanding.  
**Limitations:** Integration is not seamless in every paragraph; some financial commentary (e.g. cost-of-revenue discussion) drifts into generic profitability issues without tying back to the key concept. Because the concept is not fully embedded âthroughoutâ, the work falls short of the fully-integrated excellence expected for the top band.

annotations.comment: Description of revenue growth calls a 70% rise âexponential,â which is mathematically inaccurateâadjust terminology and verify percentage.
The title page clearly presents the research question and key concept, providing a strong focus for the report.
The word count detail is administrative; it should not appear in the body text section. Keep word count on the title page only.
The table of contents is well organized with clear headings, supporting logical navigation through the report.
Consider adding subsection page numbers for the table of contents to improve reader orientation.
The introductory paragraph provides context but lacks specific metrics or industry benchmarks to situate Teslaâs market position.
The introduction articulates the research question clearly, reinforcing alignment with the investigationâs focus.
Methodology table lists relevant documents, but adding at least one independent industry report would deepen the evidence base.
The methodology narrative mentions objectivity but could explain criteria for source selection more explicitly.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The labeling of Figure 3 and Figure 4 in the body refers to appendices, which can confuse readers; integrate key figures directly in analysis.
Linking increased cost of revenue to R&D investment shows good conceptual integration, reinforcing sustainabilityâs trade-off with profit.
The second paragraph asserts market dominance using âFigure 2â but lacks citation details for the data source. Add precise reference.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Consider including ratio analysis (e.g. gross margin trends) to strengthen financial evaluation and contextualize profitability changes.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The CSR definition is concise and sets up the discussion well, showing clear understanding of the tool.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The description of Figure 1 relies on company-provided data; a critical evaluation of methodology in the report would strengthen depth.
The claim of 90% customer satisfaction from Forbes needs an inline citation and context on sample and methodology.
The stakeholder theory discussion connects well to CSR, demonstrating integration of theory and concept.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The transition sentence at the end of the CSR section is incomplete, suggesting a missing continuation of thought.
The SWOT introduction effectively sets the stage for analysis but could preview key findings to guide the reader.
Strengths paragraph is cohesive but cuts off mid-sentence; complete the thought and integrate specific evidence.
The weaknesses section robustly addresses supply chain risks, but would benefit from quantitative evidence to underscore impact.
In the opportunities discussion, integrating risk analysis for market expansion would balance the outlook.
Threats section ends abruptly without fully articulating competitor risk. Extend analysis to complete the argument.
Conclusion on page 12 effectively synthesizes findings but repeats on page 13; consolidate to avoid redundancy and maintain coherence.
Final bibliography includes required elements, but consistency formatting (e.g., DOI vs. URL) could be improved for presentation.
Appendix tables are comprehensive, but critical insights should be drawn in the main text rather than relegated to appendices.
